messages.subject: [R-sig-teaching] R-sig-teaching Digest, Vol 30, Issue 3
messages.body_plain:
Here's a good exposition on the topics:

http://cran.r-project.org/doc/contrib/Ricci-distributions-en.pdf

Hope it helps.

Regards,

Chi Yau
http://r-tutor.com



r-sig-teaching-request at r-project.org wrote:
> Send R-sig-teaching mailing list submissions to
> 	r-sig-teaching at r-project.org
>
> To subscribe or unsubscribe via the World Wide Web, visit
> 	https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-teaching
> or, via email, send a message with subject or body 'help' to
> 	r-sig-teaching-request at r-project.org
>
> You can reach the person managing the list at
> 	r-sig-teaching-owner at r-project.org
>
> When replying, please edit your Subject line so it is more specific
> than "Re: Contents of R-sig-teaching digest..."
>
>
> Today's Topics:
>
>    1. Help with Goodness of Fit Test (AbouEl-Makarim Aboueissa)
>
>
> ----------------------------------------------------------------------
>
> Message: 1
> Date: Wed, 28 Apr 2010 16:23:40 -0400
> From: "AbouEl-Makarim Aboueissa" <aaboueissa at usm.maine.edu>
> To: <R-sig-teaching at r-project.org>
> Subject: [R-sig-teaching] Help with Goodness of Fit Test
> Message-ID: <4BD8610C020000A600072017 at uct5.uct.usm.maine.edu>
> Content-Type: text/plain
>
> Dear All:
>  
> Can someone help me how to conduct a Chi-square goodness of fit test in R. Also how to use for both discrete and continuous data.
>  
> For examples: how to test that the underlying distribution is Weibull or Poisson or Binomial, etc...
>  
> Whoever willing to help, please use any artificial data sets.
>  
> With many thanks
> Abou
>  
>  
>  
> ==========================
> AbouEl-Makarim Aboueissa, Ph.D.
> Assistant Professor of Statistics
> Department of Mathematics & Statistics
